2605.22822.title: Bottom-up open EFT for non-Abelian gauge theory with dynamical color environment
2605.22822.summary:
- • Developed bottom-up open EFT for non-Abelian gauge theories within Schwinger-Keldysh formalism.
- • Retained slow environmental response variables for local system-environment EFT construction.
- • Resulting EFT provides gauge-covariant Markov embedding of nonlocal color response.

2605.22807.title: How many systems can be dephased before the quantum switch becomes causally definite?
2605.22807.summary:
- • Dephasing all systems makes bipartite processes causally separable.
- • Keeping future system undephased preserves causal nonseparability in bipartite processes.
- • Dephasing all systems makes multipartite QC-QCs causally separable.

2605.22797.title: One-photon communication in atomic media
2605.22797.summary:
- • Single-photon transmission through atomic media is considered in the paper.
- • Quantum channel fidelity measures information loss in the process.
- • Normalized fidelity decreases with coupling strength in atomic media.

2605.22796.title: Geometric Origin of the Non-Adiabaticity Parameter and Self-Limiting Instability in Driven Nonlinear Systems
2605.22796.summary:
- • Non-adiabaticity parameter has a geometric interpretation in projective Hilbert space.
- • Fubini Study metric provides a local geometric criterion for instability.
- • Nonlinear regulator suppresses effective geometric evolution speed in driven systems.

2605.22770.title: Adiabatic Quantum Phase Estimation
2605.22770.summary:
- • Adiabatic protocol estimates eigenvalues up to precision ε.
- • Heisenberg-limited time scaling achieved in precision and failure probability.
- • Robust against dephasing errors due to population encoding.

2605.22758.title: A sharp interaction-degree threshold for simulating QAOA
2605.22758.summary:
- • Classical simulation of QAOA collapses the polynomial hierarchy at degree 3.
- • Exact classical sampling is possible at degree 2 with certain conditions.
- • Hard degree-3 instances have trivially optimizable cost functions.

2605.22744.title: Quantum circuit design via dynamic Pauli constraints
2605.22744.summary:
- • Gates are specified by constraints expressed in Pauli observables.
- • Model is equivalent to the coupling-graph-restricted circuit model.
- • Simulating a depth-D circuit requires O(D^2 N log N) complexity.

2605.22713.title: Self-testing of exact entanglement embezzlement
2605.22713.summary:
- • Exact entanglement embezzlement protocols involve unitaries or contractions.
- • Protocols arise from a unique state on the Cuntz algebra tensor product.
- • Embezzlement is a self-test for Cuntz isometries and a quasi-free state.

2605.22603.title: Sudden death of entanglement, rebirth of magic
2605.22603.summary:
- • Local noise cannot restore entanglement but can bring back magic.
- • Stabilizer membership is not preserved by local channels and noise.
- • Magic and entanglement exhibit a complementary relationship under damping.

2605.22583.title: Optimal work extraction in measurement-based quantum Otto engines: Non-adiabaticity and generalized measurements can be beneficial
2605.22583.summary:
- • Measurement-based quantum Otto engines offer greater controllability and simpler implementation.
- • POVM-based engines can yield higher optimal work extraction than PVM-based ones.
- • Non-adiabatic implementations can yield higher work output and efficiency than adiabatic ones.

2605.22764.title: Spin Glass Mapping of the Parallel Minority Game
2605.22764.summary:
- • The parallel minority game is a complex combinatorial optimization problem.
- • It is equivalent to finding the ground state of an Ising spin glass.
- • The variance is a quadratic Hamiltonian with random couplings and fields.

2605.22691.title: Posterior Collapse as Automatic Spectral Pruning
2605.22691.summary:
- • Posterior collapse in β-VAEs implements automatic spectral pruning.
- • Latent modes collapse if contribution to reconstruction is below β cutoff.
- • Collapse thresholds identify effective variables to inspect first in dataset.

2605.22646.title: Directed extended-range percolation
2605.22646.summary:
- • Directionality reduces complexity of percolation in networks with paths of length R ≥ 2.
- • DERP enables exact determination of percolation threshold and critical indices.
- • Critical behavior of DERP depends sensitively on degree correlations in networks.

2605.22555.title: Entanglement Dynamics across a Monitored Quantum Point Contact
2605.22555.summary:
- • Entanglement entropy grows linearly in time up to a maximum value.
- • Linear growth arises from an emergent bias voltage established by losses.
- • Entanglement decays to zero as the system empties out slowly.

2605.22520.title: The cell fluid model with Curie-Weiss interactions: special cases and analytical results
2605.22520.summary:
- • The model combines global attraction and local repulsion interactions between particles.
- • Analytical results are provided for the ideal-gas and strong-repulsion limits.
- • The equation of state matches the van der Waals lattice gas model.

2605.22424.title: Long-range nonstabilizerness of topologically encoded states from mutual information
2605.22424.summary:
- • Mutual information diagnoses long-range nonstabilizerness in topological systems.
- • Analysis of non-overlapping regions with non-contractible loops is crucial.
- • Mutual information classifies LRN in the toric code model perfectly.

2605.22383.title: Aggregation-Fragmentation Processes with Broken Detailed Balance
2605.22383.summary:
- • Aggregation-fragmentation processes break detailed balance under certain conditions.
- • Steady states can be deduced from an exact Laplace transform solution.
- • Fragmentation rates scaling as (mass)^β affect steady state behavior.

2605.22103.title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless-type Transition in Site Percolation on the Diamond Hierarchical Lattice
2605.22103.summary:
- • Site percolation on the diamond lattice does not undergo a transition phase.
- • The system exhibits a nonpercolating phase for p < pc and a critical phase.
- • The correlation length exhibits a Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless-type essential singularity.

2605.21933.title: Thermodynamic Irreversibility of Training Algorithms
2605.21933.summary:
- • Training algorithms introduce far-from-equilibrium dynamical processes in AI systems.
- • Four irreversibility characterizations are equivalent to leading order in step size.
- • Irreversibility breaks non-isometric symmetries and minimizes entropy production rate.

2605.21844.title: A Utility-Driven Bounded-Confidence Model for Opinion Dynamics
2605.21844.summary:
- • Opinions with higher utility exert stronger social influence in the model.
- • Mean opinion follows a stochastic differential equation in single-cluster regime.
- • Multimodal utility functions lead to metastability and opinion switching.

2605.22299.title: Data-Driven Reduced Modeling of Delayed Dynamical Systems via Spectral Submanifolds
2605.22299.summary:
- • Data-driven model reduction of nonlinear delay systems is enabled by SSM theory.
- • SSM-reduced models remain predictive even for chaotic dynamics and bifurcations.
- • No information about DDE form or delay magnitude is required for reduction.

2605.21628.title: What We Talk About When We Talk About Dissipative Quantum Chaos
2605.21628.summary:
- • Dissipative quantum chaos extends Hamiltonian quantum chaos to open systems.
- • New theory provides tools to distinguish chaotic open systems.
- • Theory is based on spectral properties of open quantum operators.

2605.21357.title: Physical completion of the Navier-Stokes equations
2605.21357.summary:
- • The Navier-Stokes equations can be derived without linearisation assumptions.
- • The nonlinear convective term drops out of the Fokker-Planck equilibrium condition.
- • The resulting stochastic system is globally well-posed and converges exponentially.

2605.22793.title: Variation of Venusian Gravity Wave Absolute Momentum Fluxes and Drag as Retrieved from the Akatsuki Mission
2605.22793.summary:
- Temperature perturbations reach amplitudes of approximately ±10 K on Venus.
- Momentum flux increases exponentially below 50-60 km, then peaks and attenuates.
- Wave drag becomes prominent where momentum flux begins to decrease.

2605.22710.title: Dynamics of fast magnetosonic wave turbulence
2605.22710.summary:
- Fast magnetosonic waves are fundamental oscillation modes of astrophysical plasmas.
- Numerical simulations study the dynamics of wave turbulence kinetic equation.
- Kolmogorov-Zakharov spectrum is found in the radial direction with anisotropy.

2605.22648.title: Experimental investigation of twin pulsed jets in a hemispheric elastic cavity
2605.22648.summary:
- Two pulsed jets interact within an elastic hemispheric cavity.
- Jet strength and spacing ratio affect fluid dynamics within cavity.
- Three distinct flow regimes are observed in the experiment.

2605.22546.title: Perpendicular rod-airfoil aeroacoustics: experiments and modelling of interaction noise
2605.22546.summary:
- A simplified configuration of a cylinder and airfoil is studied experimentally.
- Simultaneous acoustic and particle image velocimetry measurements are taken.
- Powell-Howe vortex-sound theory is combined with Green function for modelling.

2605.22326.title: Lagrangian single-particle, multi-particle and topological analyses in turbulent Rayleigh-Bénard convection
2605.22326.summary:
- Particle accelerations show robust statistics and Heisenberg-Yaglom behaviour.
- Lagrangian heat transfer is extremely intermittent and highly variable.
- Topological analysis identifies dust-devil-like convective vortices in the Q-R plane.

2605.22274.title: A unified gas-kinetic wave-particle method for multiscale binary-species gas mixtures
2605.22274.summary:
- UGKWP method decomposes gas distribution function into hydrodynamic waves and particles.
- Method recovers correct viscosity and diffusion coefficients in continuum flow regime.
- UGKWP method incorporates Shakhov model to correct Prandtl number and heat conduction.

2605.22214.title: N-Component Free Energy Lattice Boltzmann Method with Reduction Consistency and Global Momentum Conservation
2605.22214.summary:
- Model simulates fluid systems with arbitrary number of immiscible components.
- Method is strictly reduction consistent to prevent spontaneous nucleation.
- Novel discretization conserves momentum to machine precision globally.

2605.22212.title: Global exponential stability for the three-dimensional Navier-Stokes equations on hyperbolic space
2605.22212.summary:
- The Navier-Stokes equations admit a unique global mild solution on hyperbolic space.
- Exponential decay rate is μλ_\Def^{(}, where λ_\Def^{( = 26/
- Negative curvature provides a spectral gap for exponential stability.

2605.22107.title: Full Turbulence Simulation of Channel Flow at $Re_τ \approx 1000$
2605.22107.summary:
- FTS of channel flow at Re_tau ≈ 1000 was performed with high fidelity.
- Wall-normal grid spacing confirmed no adverse numerical effects at high resolution.
- First-approximation DNS resolution requires Delta x+ ≈ 19 and Delta y+ ≈

2605.22042.title: Modelling hydroelastic flexure of arbitrarily shaped ice shelves forced by long ocean waves
2605.22042.summary:
- A hydroelastic model is developed for wave-induced ice shelf flexure.
- The model allows for ice shelves of arbitrary shape and thickness.
- Finite elements and a Dirichlet-to-Neumann map are used for computation.

2605.22242.title: Decomposing Ensemble Spread in Lorenz '96 With Learned Stochastic Parameterizations
2605.22242.summary:
- Ensemble perturbations regulate trajectory decorrelation and exploration.
- Stochastic parameterizations enhance early spread growth and consistency.
- Long-term variance is not increased by ensemble perturbations.

2605.21875.title: Water vapor buoyancy and the African easterly jet
2605.21875.summary:
- African easterly jet's magnitude reduced by 30% due to moisture gradient.
- Vapor buoyancy counteracts temperature gradient's effect on density balance.
- Global warming strengthens effect of vapor buoyancy on African easterly jet.

2605.21864.title: A Simulation Methodology Testbed for Typhoon Sensitivity Analysis: Framework Development and Perturbation-Response Experiments with the Pangu Weather Model
2605.21864.summary:
- A simulation testbed is developed for typhoon sensitivity analysis using the Pangu model.
- The testbed integrates AI forecasting with PID closed-loop techniques for analysis.
- Experiments reveal perturbation-response ranges and input-output coupling relationships.

2605.21790.title: Microwave-Stimulated Serpentinization of Olivine for Geological Hydrogen Production
2605.21790.summary:
- Microwave stimulation accelerates serpentinization of olivine at atmospheric pressure.
- Hydrogen concentration increased 12-fold with microwave exposure compared to hot-plate.
- Microwave stimulation enhances serpentinization kinetics through rapid volumetric heating.

2605.21687.title: Three-Dimensional Velocity Analysis and Particle Size Dynamics from Multi-Site RGB-Photometry of Noctilucent Clouds
2605.21687.summary:
- Method measures altitude and particle size of noctilucent clouds using RGB-photometry.
- Three velocity components and particle radius are determined from observational data.
- Meridional motion drives change in particle size of noctilucent clouds.

2605.21673.title: From Licensing to Open Access: Designing a Sustainable Transition in Operational Weather Data
2605.21673.summary:
- ECMWF transitioned from restricted data licensing to open access in October
- Tiered service model kept core forecast data open with cost-recovered delivery.
- 93% of paying organisations retained Service Agreement after transition.

2605.21196.title: Effect of grid anisotropy, resolution, and subgrid-scale models in pseudo-spectral Large Eddy Simulations of low-level clouds
2605.21196.summary:
- Grid anisotropy improves accuracy and computational efficiency in simulations.
- AMD model with pseudo-spectral advection produces robust predictions without tuning.
- Grid anisotropy balances accuracy and efficiency in simulations effectively.

2605.21191.title: Beyond Vorticity: An Angular Momentum Perspective on Fluid Flow
2605.21191.summary:
- Angular momentum density field provides a complementary framework to vorticity analysis.
- Viscous torque decomposes into diffusive and local spin dissipative terms.
- Hydrodynamic impulse reformulates into dilatational, volumetric, and rotational flux components.

2605.20925.title: Blending machine learning and physics-based approaches for weather and climate: a typology
2605.20925.summary:
- Machine learning and physics-based models are integrated for weather and climate prediction.
- Blended approaches combine benefits of machine learning and physics-based systems.
- Typology provides framework for informed decision-making and strategic planning.

2605.21174.title: Exact expression for maximum Lyapunov exponent during transients in computationally powerful dynamical networks
2605.21174.summary:
- Network's time dynamics can be exactly solved through nonlinear transformation.
- Exact analytical expression for maximum Lyapunov exponent is derived.
- Network exhibits positive MLEs during transients useful for computation.

2605.21530.title: Pairwise Distance-Diffusion Analysis (PDDA): A Geometric Framework for Estimating Hurst Exponents in Multivariate Long-Memory Processes
2605.21530.summary:
- PDDA is a geometric framework for estimating Hurst exponents.
- PDDA yields two complementary routes: R/S-PDDA and MSD-PDDA analysis.
- PDDA is extended to multivariate isotropic and anisotropic processes analysis.

2605.23894.title: A Two-Branch Finite-Field Construction for Regular CSS LDPC Bases
2605.23894.summary:
- A two-branch construction reduces regularity and CSS orthogonality conditions.
- The construction separates design into two stages: base matrix and lift.
- The resulting CSS code has girth at least eight and excludes specific orbits.

2605.23881.title: Time crystals in cavity-BEC systems
2605.23881.summary:
- Time crystals in cavity-BEC systems are reviewed and discussed.
- Three distinct time crystalline states are predicted and realized.
- Cavity mode and condensate momentum modes are used for characterization.

2605.23880.title: Not all black holes decohere quantum superpositions
2605.23880.summary:
- Near-extremal charged black holes exhibit large quantum metric fluctuations.
- Quantum gravity effects make decoherence rate vanish at late times.
- Decoherence rate is suppressed relative to semiclassical expectations always.

2605.23874.title: Quantum Quenches that Resemble Operator Growth
2605.23874.summary:
- Linear growth of Lanzcos coefficients conjectured in generic quenches.
- Growth quench dynamics localized in Krylov and Fock spaces.
- Fock space cage states interpreted as conserved charge in East-West model.

2605.23873.title: Coherent dynamics in chaotic spin chains via interference-protected subspaces
2605.23873.summary:
- Local spin-1/2 chains exhibit nonthermal phenomena at high energy densities.
- Structured subspaces are protected by destructive interference in these chains.
- A leakage theory predicts states that retain coherence in these chains.

2605.23862.title: Indefinite probabilities in quantum spacetime: A deepening of unpredictability
2605.23862.summary:
- Non-commutative spacetime captures non-classical features of spacetime and its symmetries.
- SU_q( quantum group leads to non-commuting probability operators for spin measurements.
- Indefinite probabilities result from uncertainty principle between different probability operators.

2605.23853.title: Exact versus tight-binding models in longitudinally modulated $\mathcal{PT}$-symmetric coupled waveguides
2605.23853.summary:
- Exact continuous solutions are derived from z-dependent supersymmetric transformations.
- Tight-binding model is widely adopted but not rigorously benchmarked against exact solutions.
- TB model reproduces spatial intensity distributions but not complex phase dynamics.

2605.23852.title: Convexity and non-Markovianity of Weyl Maps
2605.23852.summary:
- Weyl maps exhibit non-Markovian dynamics in finite-dimensional open systems.
- Convex combinations of Weyl maps can generate Markovian semigroups.
- Non-Markovianity is not additive under mixing of Weyl maps.

2605.23830.title: IntegrateUnitary.jl: A Julia package for symbolic integration over Haar measures
2605.23830.summary:
- IntegrateUnitary.jl is a Julia package for symbolic integration over Haar measures.
- It supports compact groups, circular and Gaussian ensembles, and Ginibre ensembles.
- Package provides Weingarten calculus and Wick contractions with symbolic-$d$ support.

2605.23824.title: The negativity core of a 1+1D massless scalar quantum field
2605.23824.summary:
- Vacuum entanglement is a fundamental feature of quantum field theory.
- Entanglement between regions is characterized in a 1+1D scalar field.
- Negativity cores are localized modes carrying logarithmic negativity in the field.

2605.23803.title: Chirality-sensitive mobility and dissipation of Brownian motion on a helical landscape
2605.23803.summary:
- Brownian motion separates into free propagation and harmonic motion in a helical landscape.
- Anisotropic damping destroys separability in the stochastic dynamics of the particle.
- Zero mode associated with diffusion along the screw coordinate is projected out.

2605.23789.title: Orientable Surfactants on Thin Liquid Films: A Dynamic Density-Functional Theory Approach
2605.23789.summary:
- Surfactants are treated as polar uniaxial particles in the model.
- Dynamic density-functional theory is used for surfactant-laden films.
- Thin-film equations are derived under the long-wave approximation.

2605.23781.title: Multi-field Return Point Memory
2605.23781.summary:
- Non-equilibrium systems display memory beyond their present environment.
- Multiple control fields enable precise and complex system control.
- Return-point memory restores a system to a previous microstate.

2605.23758.title: Order-Disorder Tricriticality in $\mathrm{A}_n \mathrm{B}_n$ Star Polymer Melts
2605.23758.summary:
- Arm number drives order-disorder transition from second to first order.
- Tricritical arm number is approximately 4475 in star-polymer melts.
- Transition becomes first order for arm numbers above

2605.23567.title: Phase diagram of the vortex state in an amorphous Re6Zr thin film exhibiting inverse melting
2605.23567.summary:
- Vortex lattice exhibits inverse melting in amorphous Re6Zr thin film.
- Inverse melting transitions from liquid to crystalline solid with temperature.
- Phase diagram constructed in magnetic field-temperature parameter space.

2605.23364.title: Inviscid scaling in the Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation from functional renormalization group and direct numerical simulations
2605.23364.summary:
- The Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation features a scaling regime with dynamical exponent z=
- This scaling regime arises from the vanishing of effective viscosity in KS.
- The behavior pertains to the inviscid-Burgers universality class with z=1 scaling.

2605.23285.title: Reinforcement Learning for Microcanonical Graph Ensemble with Assortativity Constraints
2605.23285.summary:
- DMGG uses reinforcement learning to generate graphs with exact assortativity constraints.
- DMGG eliminates exhaustive parameter tuning and accelerates graph generation significantly.
- DMGG provides exact null models for quantitative analysis of graph observables.

2605.23228.title: Exact solution of generalized gauge-invariant Ising chains with multi-spin interactions
2605.23228.summary:
- Exact solutions are obtained for generalized gauge-invariant n-chain Ising models.
- Explicit expression for the partition function is derived using transfer-matrix method.
- Wilson loop behavior exhibits area-law and perimeter-law dependence regimes.

2605.23877.title: Particle Image Velocimetry of 3D printed vascular fluidic phantom devices
2605.23877.summary:
- 3D printed vascular models were fabricated with straight and pathological geometries.
- Flow experiments were conducted under steady laminar conditions using microPIV.
- Measured velocities were compared to analytical Hagen Poiseuille predictions with errors.

2605.23869.title: Soft Mobility Theory
2605.23869.summary:
- Soft mobility theory applies virtual power and Lorentz reciprocal theorem.
- Soft mobility equation depends on instantaneous deformation and deformation.
- Framework allows efficient gradient-based inverse design and simulation.

2605.23812.title: An Ensemble Variational approach for High-Dimensional Open-Loop Flow Control
2605.23812.summary:
- Ensemble-variational framework approximates cost-function gradients through perturbed control vectors.
- Method uses finite-difference approximation in ensemble space for high-dimensional parameter spaces.
- Framework remains effective in chaotic regime where adjoint-based approaches fail.

2605.23800.title: A derivation of viscous thin film flow equations on curved surfaces
2605.23800.summary:
- Viscous thin film flow equations are derived for curved surfaces.
- Derivation includes gravitational, capillary, and Marangoni effects.
- Resulting expression incorporates leading order terms and additional terms.

2605.23729.title: Real time monitoring of pressure-induced deformation of PDMS to evaluate pressure distribution in microfluidic channels
2605.23729.summary:
- Real-time pressure measurements are essential for microfluidic applications.
- Existing approaches require additional probes or sensing elements.
- Compliant microfluidic channels are used for pressure sensing.

2605.22909.title: Sample-efficient benchmarking of shallow all-to-all random quantum circuits
2605.22909.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed a sample-efficient benchmark for shallow-depth random quantum circuits.
- Nonlinear cross-entropy and heavy output generation were used to distinguish between noisy quantum computers and classical spoofers.

2605.22915.title: Unified resonant-manifold framework for dynamical quantum phase transitions
2605.22915.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers propose a unified framework for dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs).
- The framework distinguishes between manifold and branch DQPTs based on resonances within and between state manifolds.

2605.22917.title: Quantum Fisher Information under decoherence with explicit wavefunctions
2605.22917.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed a method to estimate quantum Fisher information (QFI) in many-body systems under decoherence.
- The method maps QFI lower bounds onto expectation values over a classical probability distribution, enabling efficient estimation via Monte Carlo sampling.

2605.22920.title: Estimating Green's functions with a robust quantum Arnoldi method
2605.22920.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A robust quantum Arnoldi method (ROQAM) is introduced for estimating Green's functions.
- ROQAM preserves the upper-Hessenberg structure of projected matrices and reduces precision requirements.

2605.22922.title: Observation of associative-memory retrieval and spin-glass phases on a photonic quantum simulator
2605.22922.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers used a photonic quantum simulator to study associative memories and neural networks.
- The simulator identified three distinct phases: memory retrieval, spin-glass "black-out," and paramagnetic.

2605.22947.title: Entanglement-facilitated macroscopic cluster formation in quantum many-body dynamics
2605.22947.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Quantum many-body systems can preserve global information through non-local correlations.
- Initial-state entanglement helps stabilize macroscopic cluster structures in 2D quantum Ising models.

2605.22953.title: Chaos to Synchronization and Dissipative Quantum Scarring in Open Coupled top-Dicke model in a Lossy Cavity
2605.22953.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- The open coupled-top Dicke model explores non-equilibrium phenomena, including quantum scars in an open environment.
- Photon loss induces spontaneous synchronization and chaos, followed by restoration of coherence and synchronization.

2605.22966.title: Phase-dependent role of dissipation across the Aubry-Andr\'e-Harper transition
2605.22966.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Non-Markovian dissipation affects transport across the Aubry-Andr\'e-Harper transition.
- In the extended phase, dissipation qualitatively reshapes the dynamical generator.

2605.22977.title: Absorbing Many-Body Correlations into Core-Optimized Orbitals
2605.22977.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed a method called Core-Optimized Orbitals (COO) to optimize orbitals in Configuration Interaction (CI) simulations.
- COO reduces the number of determinants required in CI simulations by several orders of magnitude, making it more computationally efficient.

2605.22980.title: Automatic De-Quantization of Quantum Programs Using Constant Propagation
2605.22980.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers propose a method to reduce quantum operations by replacing them with classical instructions.
- The approach, called hybrid quantum-classical constant propagation, eliminates unnecessary quantum gates and controls.

2605.22933.title: Emergent heavy-tailed distributions from a Markovian random walk
2605.22933.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A Markovian random walk with a deterministic step length function exhibits heavy-tailed distributions.
- The walk's positive feedback loop induces strong correlations, leading to a non-Gaussian stationary state.

2605.22906.title: Weak wave turbulence as a precursor to universal coarsening in a homogeneous Bose gas
2605.22906.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A homogeneous Bose gas exhibits universal coarsening behavior.
- Weak wave turbulence describes the initial transport of particles to low momenta.

2605.22982.title: Transient and asymptotic Taylor--Aris dispersion of Brownian rods in arbitrary regular-polygonal ducts
2605.22982.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Brownian rods in non-circular ducts exhibit unique dispersion behavior due to pressure-driven shear alignment.
- The dispersion is governed by a tensorial diffusion that varies across the duct cross-section, influenced by the duct geometry.

2605.23046.title: Weakly nonlinear interaction of capillary waves in a finite system: leading interaction process and scales' range of direct energy cascade
2605.23046.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Resonant interactions dominate the weakly nonlinear interaction of capillary waves.
- The Zakharov-Filonenko spectrum forms in isotropic turbulence of capillary waves.

2605.23166.title: Free surfaces in turbulence -- A unified framework from water surfaces to elastic solids
2605.23166.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A unified framework is developed to study deformable surfaces in turbulent flows.
- The framework predicts distinct dynamical regimes depending on whether surface dynamics emerge or are enslaved by flow turbulence.

2605.23303.title: On the Applicability of the Gas-Kinetic Scheme with Kinetic Boundary Conditions for Near-Continuum Hypersonic Flows
2605.23303.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- The Gas-Kinetic Scheme (GKS) is applied to near-continuum hypersonic flows.
- GKS with kinetic boundary conditions captures non-equilibrium physics and velocity slip.

2605.23309.title: Full-component reconstruction of three-dimensional fluid stress tensors
2605.23309.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed U-FlowPET, a framework to reconstruct 3D fluid stress tensors from optical measurements.
- U-FlowPET integrates photoelastic tomography with fluid mechanics equations to find physically admissible stress fields.

2605.23396.title: CHIMERA: A wide Reynolds number range Taylor-Couette facility
2605.23396.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers designed a Taylor-Couette facility (CHIMERA) for studying angular momentum transport across a wide Reynolds number range.
- The facility uses a novel torque measurement technique, suspending the outer cylinder as a torsion pendulum, for direct inference of fluid-induced torque.

2605.23620.title: From Optical Breakdown to Bubble Inception: A Coupled Plasma-Thermal Framework for Nanosecond Laser-Induced Cavitation in Water
2605.23620.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A coupled plasma-thermal framework is developed to explain nanosecond laser-induced cavitation in water.
- Plasma-induced thermoelastic acoustic relaxation drives initial bubble formation, while residual thermal energy sustains growth.

2605.23776.title: Precipitation diffusion downscaling and application to out-of-distribution simulations with and without stratospheric aerosol injection
2605.23776.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed a deep learning diffusion downscaler to generate high-resolution precipitation data from coarse climate simulations.
- The downscaler accurately recreated climate change projections of extreme precipitation and generated realistic downscaled precipitation data.

2605.23778.title: The physics of AI weather models
2605.23778.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- AI weather models may be solving physical equations, not those used by conventional NWP models.
- Different AI models represent the atmosphere similarly, despite varying architectures and capacities.

2605.23875.title: Atmosphere as a steam engine
2605.23875.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- The Earth's atmosphere operates a steam cycle where water vapor evaporates, expands, condenses, and returns as precipitation.
- The paper estimates the global "steam-engine power" as 4 ± 9 W/m², close to the total atmospheric power.

2605.23403.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Corrective Diffusion Modeling for Meteorological Downscaling
2605.23403.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A hybrid quantum-classical model is proposed for statistical downscaling of weather fields.
- The model inserts quantum circuit layers into a diffusion UNet to act as compact nonlinear feature maps.

2412.00936.title: Smoothing and spatial verification of global fields
2412.00936.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed two new methodologies for smoothing global fields to enable spatial verification in global domains.
- The methods can handle varying grid point area sizes and missing data, making them suitable for high-resolution global fields.

2503.05331.title: Radiosonde-constrained reconstructions reveal a weakening Northern Hadley circulation
2503.05331.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- The Northern Hadley cell (NHC) has shown a weakening trend since 1980, according to radiosonde-constrained reconstructions.
- Climate models consistently simulate a weakening NHC, but reanalyses have suggested an opposing strengthening trend.

2601.05841.title: Non-stationary time series attribution for heatwaves over Europe
2601.05841.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed a method to attribute entire time series of extreme events to anthropogenic climate change.
- The method uses a non-stationary Markov process and bivariate extreme value theory to model temporal dependence.

2605.11639.title: Enabling High-Accuracy Data Assimilation with Limited Ensembles via Machine Learning-Based Covariance Correction
2605.11639.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A machine learning-based framework is proposed to improve ensemble Kalman filter (EnKF) accuracy with limited ensemble sizes.
- A multilayer perceptron (MLP) predicts the difference between forecast error covariances from limited and large ensembles.

2605.22901.title: A No-Go Theorem for the Mass-Radius Relation of Solitons
2605.22901.summary:
- Here are three concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- A no-go theorem is proven for the mass-radius relation of solitons, excluding typical non-topological, non-relativistic, and spherically symmetric solitons from having a mass-radius index (Γ) in the range [0, d].
- The theorem applies to real scalar field theories in d spatial dimensions, considering forces from gradient energy, self-interaction, and gravitation, and works for arbitrary self-interactions.

2605.23001.title: Nonlinear Wave Propagation in 1D Polycatenated Ring Chains
2605.23001.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers studied nonlinear wave dynamics in 1D chains of polycatenated rings.
- The system exhibits amplitude-dependent nonlinear wave propagation due to internal flexibility.

2605.23688.title: Adjoint inverse design of microresonator frequency combs
2605.23688.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers developed an adjoint-based inverse-design framework for microresonator frequency combs.
- This approach optimizes comb spectra directly, reducing reliance on manual or semi-empirical design methods.

2605.22236.title: Beyond descendants: integrable observables for cohomological field theories
2605.22236.summary:
- Here are 3 concise bullet points summarizing the paper:
- Researchers propose "integrable observables" as alternatives to Witten's psi classes in cohomological field theories.
- Three examples of integrable observables are presented, recovering and extending known hierarchies.
